workflow.name: Monitor legal policy changes with Google Sheets, Gmail and GPT-4o
workflow.description:
## Terms of Service Change Watcher with AI Summaries
Automatically monitors Terms of Service, Privacy Policy, and other legal pages for changes. When content changes are detected, GPT-4o analyzes the old vs new text and emails you a plain-English summary with an impact rating.

### How it works
1. Every day at 8 AM, the workflow reads your list of URLs from a Google Sheet
2. Each page is fetched and stripped to plain text
3. The text is compared against the previously stored version
4. If a change is detected, the old and new content are sent to GPT-4o for analysis
5. You receive an email with a summary of exactly what changed and how it impacts you
6. All changes are logged to a Change Log sheet for a historical record

### Setup
1. Create a Google Sheet with two tabs: **Pages** (columns: `url`, `page_name`, `last_content`, `last_checked`) and **Change Log** (columns: `date`, `page_name`, `url`, `summary`)
2. Add the URLs you want to monitor in the Pages tab — leave `last_content` and `last_checked` empty
3. Connect your **Google Sheets OAuth2**, **Gmail OAuth2**, and **OpenAI API** credentials
4. Update the Google Sheet document in all four Google Sheets nodes
5. Set your email address in the Send Change Alert node
6. Run once to save baselines, then activate the workflow

workflow.name: Terms of Service Change Watcher with AI Summaries

parameters.text:
=You are a legal document analyst. You MUST compare the two text versions below word-by-word and identify ONLY real, verifiable differences. Do NOT guess or generalize. Every change you list must quote the actual text that was added, removed, or modified.

Page: {{ $json.page_name }} ({{ $json.url }})

--- OLD VERSION ---
{{ $json.old_content_for_ai }}

--- NEW VERSION ---
{{ $json.new_content_for_ai }}

Rules:
- ONLY report changes you can verify by comparing the two texts above
- Quote the specific text that changed (use "was: ..." and "now: ..." format)
- If large sections were added or removed, summarize what was added/removed with a brief quote
- If you cannot find concrete differences, say so honestly
- NEVER fabricate or assume changes that are not visible in the text

Respond in this format:

**Overview:** One sentence on what changed.

**Changes:**
- [Quote the specific old text → new text for each change]

**Impact Level:** LOW (cosmetic/formatting), MEDIUM (policy clarification), or HIGH (new restrictions, rights changes, data handling changes)

**What This Means For Users:** Brief practical impact in 1-2 sentences.

parameters.content:
## Terms of Service Change Watcher with AI Summaries

### How it works

1. Schedules a daily check using Daily Check. 2. Retrieves URLs from a Google Sheets workbook. 3. Fetches the specified pages and compares content. 4. Uses an AI model to summarize changes if detected. 5. Sends a change alert email and logs the change.

### Setup steps

- [ ] Set up Google Sheets credentials for retrieving and storing page data
- [ ] Configure the OpenAI Chat Model for generating AI summaries
- [ ] Set up Gmail credentials to send email alerts

### Customization

To monitor different URLs, update the Google Sheets file with new entries.

## Daily scheduling

Initiates the workflow on a daily schedule to start the monitoring process.

## Fetch pages for monitoring

Retrieves list of pages to monitor from Google Sheets and fetches each page's content.

## Content comparison

Compares the fetched content with the stored version to detect changes.

## AI-driven change summarization

If changes are detected, uses AI to summarize the differences between the current and previous content.

## Email alert and logging

Builds the email content, sends alerts via Gmail, and logs the change in Google Sheets.

## Update monitoring records

Updates the Google Sheets record to reflect the latest check time, whether changes were detected or not.

user.name: Alejandro Alfonso
user.bio: Alejandro Alfonso — Founder of Duality Labs, an AI automation agency helping businesses across industries eliminate manual work and scale smarter. I build practical n8n workflows that connect AI tools, CRMs, and communication platforms into systems that run themselves. Every template I publish is production-tested and built with real client problems in mind.

data.details:
The IF node can be used to implement binary conditional logic in your workflow. You can set up one-to-many conditions to evaluate each item of data being inputted into the node. That data will either evaluate to TRUE or FALSE and route out of the node accordingly.

This node has multiple types of conditions: Bool, String, Number, and Date & Time.
